sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Focus on recent transitions only
print("=== Recent transitions (last 5) ===")
for t in transitions[-5:]:
    step = t.after_frame.step
    action = t.action
    before_seg = t.before_frame.segmentation
    after_seg = t.after_frame.segmentation
    
    before_N = [n for n in before_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
    after_N = [n for n in after_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
    before_G = [n for n in before_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
    after_G = [n for n in after_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
    
    before_N_info = [(min(p[1] for p in n['boundary']), max(p[1] for p in n['boundary']), n['hash']) for n in before_N]
    after_N_info = [(min(p[1] for p in n['boundary']), max(p[1] for p in n['boundary']), n['hash']) for n in after_N]
    
    print(f"Step {step}: {action}")
    print(f"  N before: {before_N_info}")
    print(f"  N after: {after_N_info}")
    
    if before_G:
        g_info = [(min(p[1] for p in n['boundary']), max(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])) for n in before_G]
        print(f"  G before: {g_info}")
    if after_G:
        g_info = [(min(p[1] for p in n['boundary']), max(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])) for n in after_G]
        print(f"  G after: {g_info}")
    
    before_g = [n for n in before_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'g']
    after_g = [n for n in after_seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'g']
    if before_g:
        g_cols = [(min(p[1] for p in n['boundary']), max(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])) for n in before_g]
        print(f"  hollow sq before: {g_cols}")
    if after_g:
        g_cols = [(min(p[1] for p in n['boundary']), max(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])) for n in after_g]
        print(f"  hollow sq after: {g_cols}")

print("\n=== Current frame ===")
seg = current_frame.segmentation
N_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
G_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G']
g_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'g']
print(f"N nodes: {[(min(p[1] for p in n['boundary']), max(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])) for n in N_nodes]}")
print(f"G nodes: {[(min(p[1] for p in n['boundary']), max(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])) for n in G_nodes]}")
print(f"Hollow sq nodes: {[(min(p[1] for p in n['boundary']), max(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])) for n in g_nodes]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Step 26: Clicked at (row=16, col=14) - clicked on N at col 13-16. Result: G frame appeared at 12-17, hollow square appeared at cols 25-28.
Step 27: Clicked at (row=16, col=26) - clicked on the hollow square. Result: Ns at 13-16 and 19-22 disappeared, a new N appeared at 25-28. The hollow square and G frame disappeared.
So clicking the hollow square removes the selected N AND the one to its left, and creates a new N where the hollow square was!
Step 28: Clicked at (row=16, col=32) - clicked on N at col 31-34. Result: G frame at 30-35, hollow square at cols 19-22.
Step 29: Clicked at (row=16, col=26) - clicked somewhere between the hollow square (19-22) and the N at 25-28. Result: G frame moved to 24-29 (now around N at 25-28), hollow square moved to 37-40.

1. Click an N to select it (G frame appears).

Hmm, that's a bit weird. Let me look at step 25 more carefully:

- After: Ns at 13-16, 19-22, 31-34, 43-46 (top grid), 43-46 (bottom grid)
Wait, so clicking the bottom grid N somehow spawned multiple Ns in the top grid? That's strange. Let me look at the step 25 transition more carefully.

- After clicking one, the top grid got Ns at 13-16, 19-22, 31-34, 43-46 and the bottom still has one at 43-46.
Hmm, maybe the bottom grid N was a "source" that generates the pattern in the top grid?
OK let me focus on what I need to do now. Current state:
- Ns at 25-28, 31-34, 43-46 (top grid), 43-46 (bottom grid)
- G frame at 24-29 (around N at 25-28)
- Hollow square at 37-40

Wait, but in step 27, the selected N was at 13-16 and the hollow square was at 25-28. After clicking, both 13-16 and 19-22 disappeared. So it removed the selected N AND the one immediately to its right.
So the pattern seems to be:
1. Select an N (G frame appears).
2. Hollow square appears to the right of the selected N.
3. Click the hollow square to remove the selected N and the next N to its right, spawning a new N at the hollow square's position.

sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state analysis:
# - Selected N at 25-28 (G frame at 24-29)
# - Next N at 31-34
# - Hollow square at 37-40
# 
# Pattern: Click hollow square to merge selected N with next N, spawning new N at hollow square position
# 
# Let me click the hollow square center at row=16, col=38

# First let me verify the hollow square position
seg = current_frame.segmentation
g_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'g']
for n in g_nodes:
    rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
    cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
    print(f"Hollow sq piece: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# The hollow square is made of 4 g pieces at cols 37-40
# Center would be around row 16, col 38-39
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 16, 'col': 38}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
